name: Reykjavik
summary: Reykjavik is the capital of Iceland and the world's most northerly capital city. It sits between volcanoes and the cold sea, with colourful houses, steamy hot pools and a tall rocket-shaped church above the rooftops.
funFacts: Hallgrimskirkja church is Reykjavik's tallest building at 74.5 metres, and its pointy concrete tower is shaped like cooled lava and basalt rocks.
Iceland sits on top of two giant tectonic plates that are slowly pulling apart, so the island grows a few millimetres wider every single year.
The Strokkur geyser near Reykjavik blasts boiling water up to 20 metres into the sky every six to ten minutes, like a natural rocket fountain.
Reykjavik's tap water is heated underground by volcanoes, so people fill their swimming pools and even melt the snow off pavements with it.
In June the sun barely sets in Reykjavik, so kids can still see daylight at midnight during the long summer 'white nights'.
thingsToDo: Ride the lift up Hallgrimskirkja's tall rocket-like tower for sweeping views over Reykjavik's colourful rooftops.
Walk along the harbour to the shiny silver Sun Voyager sculpture that looks like a Viking longship.
Explore Harpa concert hall and watch sunlight bounce around its giant honeycomb glass walls.
Float in the warm milky-blue water of the Blue Lagoon, even when snow is falling around you.
Take a Golden Circle day trip to see Strokkur geyser erupt and giant Gullfoss waterfall thunder by.
Hop on a whale-watching boat from the Old Harbour to spot humpback and minke whales out at sea.

wildlife.name: Icelandic Horse
wildlife.description: Small fluffy horse with a thick mane and five gaits, including a super-smooth one called the tolt.

wildlife.name: Atlantic Puffin
wildlife.description: Black-and-white seabird with a bright orange beak that nests in burrows on Iceland's cliffs.

wildlife.name: Arctic Fox
wildlife.description: Iceland's only native land mammal, with a thick white winter coat to hide in the snow.

wildlife.name: Humpback Whale
wildlife.description: Huge ocean giant that breaches and slaps the water on whale-watching trips near Reykjavik.

localWord.meaning: Hello in Icelandic — easy, it sounds like the English word.

images.alt: The tall rocket-like tower of Hallgrimskirkja church rising above Reykjavik.
The shiny silver Sun Voyager sculpture shaped like a Viking longship beside Reykjavik harbour.
Harpa concert hall in Reykjavik, with its honeycomb glass walls reflecting the sky.
The shiny glass dome of Perlan sitting on top of huge hot-water tanks above Reykjavik.
Steamy milky-blue water of the Blue Lagoon geothermal spa with black lava rocks around it.
A giant jet of hot water shooting up from Strokkur geyser on Iceland's Golden Circle.
A fluffy Icelandic horse with a long mane standing in a field.
An Atlantic puffin with a bright orange beak standing on a grassy cliff in Iceland.
A snowy-white Arctic fox standing on snow in Iceland.
A humpback whale arching out of the sea on a deep dive.
